codegenInstructions: In @src/dashboard.rs around lines 305 - 340, The upsert_device currently mints and stores a new claim_token/claim_token_hash on every call (lines around mint_device_token/hash and where DeviceInfo.claim_token_hash is set), which invalidates existing tokens; change upsert_device so it first checks state.get_device(&device_id) to detect whether the device already exists and only call crate::identity::mint_device_token() and set claim_token_hash when the device is new OR when the incoming DeviceUpsertRequest includes an explicit flag (e.g., regenerate_claim_token: Option<bool>) set true; preserve the existing claim_token_hash from the existing DeviceInfo for ordinary metadata updates, and update DeviceUpsertResponse (and returned claim_token) to be optional so you only return a newly minted claim_token when one was generated.

In @docker/entrypoint.sh around lines 708 - 749, The shutdown function and its dedicated TERM trap duplicate cleanup's work and should be removed: delete the shutdown() function and the line `trap shutdown TERM`, leaving only the existing `trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM`; ensure `cleanup` (which already references PROXY_PID, COREDNS_PID, BORINGTUN_PID, WG_UP, and run_hooks PostDown) remains responsible for all termination logic so TERM signals are handled only once and no handlers are overwritten.
suggestions:
cleanup() {
	set +e

	# Signal processes first (in correct order)
	if [ -n "${PROXY_PID:-}" ]; then
		kill -TERM "$PROXY_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
	fi
	if [ -n "${COREDNS_PID:-}" ]; then
		kill -TERM "$COREDNS_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
	fi
	if [ -n "${WG_UP:-}" ]; then
		run_hooks PostDown || true
	fi
	if [ -n "${BORINGTUN_PID:-}" ]; then
		kill -TERM "$BORINGTUN_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
		wait "$BORINGTUN_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
	fi
	if [ -n "${WG_UP:-}" ]; then
		WG_UP=""
	fi
}

trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

if [ ! -f "$COREDNS_CONFIG" ]; then
	echo "missing CoreDNS config: $COREDNS_CONFIG" >&2
	exit 1
fi

ensure_wireguard_server_keys

codegenInstructions: In @sql/views.sql around lines 181 - 224, The view v_session_timeline currently joins an unbounded connection_sessions (cs) to a 7-day proxy_events aggregate (pe_agg), causing a full table scan; restrict cs to the same recent window by adding a time filter on the session timestamp(s) (e.g. cs.opened_at >= SYSTIMESTAMP - INTERVAL '7' DAY or an appropriate opened_at/closed_at overlap condition) so the FROM/WHERE that references connection_sessions limits rows to the 7-day range and aligns with pe_agg.

In @src/wg_shim.rs around lines 212 - 234, The DashMap write lock is held across await points in the sessions.entry(...) Entry::Vacant branch where UdpSocket::bind and connect are awaited; refactor to a double-checked pattern: first try sessions.get(&client_addr) and return the cloned session if present, otherwise create the UdpSocket (UdpSocket::bind and connect) and ShimSession::new outside any DashMap entry/guard, then re-open sessions.entry(client_addr) and insert the prebuilt Arc<ShimSession> only if still vacant (or fetch the existing value if another task beat us) and finally spawn run_session_receiver with the inserted/found session; this removes awaits while holding the DashMap lock and preserves the existing behavior.

In @src/lib.rs around lines 79 - 83, The current header slicing (header[..6] / header[6..]) can panic on UTF-8 boundaries; change to operate on bytes instead: check the prefix with header.as_bytes().starts_with(b"Basic ") (or equivalent byte-slice comparison) and then use header.as_bytes()[6..] (or header.as_bytes().get(6..) with handling) to obtain the encoded bytes safely before further processing; update any variables using encoded (e.g., encoded) to accept a byte slice and ensure decoding/validation uses the byte slice rather than UTF-8 index slicing.

In @docker/entrypoint.sh around lines 809 - 830, The monitoring loop restarts ssl-proxy unconditionally which can cause a tight crash loop; modify the loop around PROXY_PID/PROXY_BIN to implement a restart backoff and/or a retry limit for ssl-proxy: add a counter (e.g., proxy_restart_count) and a backoff delay (e.g., proxy_backoff_secs increasing exponentially up to a cap) before re-spawning PROXY_BIN, reset the counter on successful long-lived run, and if the retry limit is exceeded exit with a clear error message; reference BORINGTUN_PID, COREDNS_PID, PROXY_PID and PROXY_BIN when locating the restart logic to update.
suggestions:
PROXY_RESTART_COUNT=0
PROXY_RESTART_BACKOFF=1

# Monitor critical child processes
while true; do
	# Wait for any child to exit
	wait -n
	exit_code=$?

	if ! kill -0 "$BORINGTUN_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
		echo "boringtun-cli exited unexpectedly, restarting container"
		exit 1
	fi

	if ! kill -0 "$COREDNS_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
		echo "coredns exited unexpectedly, restarting container"
		exit 1
	fi

	# If ssl-proxy died, restart it
	if ! kill -0 "$PROXY_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
		PROXY_RESTART_COUNT=$((PROXY_RESTART_COUNT + 1))
		if [ "$PROXY_RESTART_COUNT" -gt 5 ]; then
			echo "ssl-proxy crashed too many times, exiting"
			exit 1
		fi
		echo "ssl-proxy exited (restart $PROXY_RESTART_COUNT), restarting in ${PROXY_RESTART_BACKOFF}s..."
		sleep "$PROXY_RESTART_BACKOFF"
		PROXY_RESTART_BACKOFF=$((PROXY_RESTART_BACKOFF * 2))
		"$PROXY_BIN" &
		PROXY_PID=$!
	fi
done

Locate remove_session_if_current, the sessions DashMap, and the ShimSession/Arc usage to implement the atomic remove_if-based check-and-delete.

In @docker/entrypoint.sh around lines 300 - 333, The configure_network_segmentation function only creates IPv4 iptables rules (chain SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT using ADMIN_PORT, WG_INTERFACE_NAME, PROXY_PORT, TPROXY_PORT, ADMIN_BIND_ADDR) so IPv6 traffic can bypass controls; add equivalent ip6tables rules (create/flush an SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT chain in ip6tables, mirror the ESTABLISHED/RELATED, lo, admin-port allow/deny, WireGuard-interface-only accept for data-plane ports and final RETURN) or alternatively document/validate that IPv6 is disabled and fail early; ensure you check for ip6tables availability similar to iptables and keep the same idempotency behavior as the existing iptables block.
suggestions:
configure_network_segmentation() {
	if ! is_truthy "$ENABLE_NETWORK_SEGMENTATION"; then
		echo "[#] Network segmentation disabled (ENABLE_NETWORK_SEGMENTATION=$ENABLE_NETWORK_SEGMENTATION)"
		return 0
	fi

	if ! command -v iptables >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		echo "[!] iptables not available; cannot enforce network segmentation" >&2
		return 1
	fi

	echo "[#] Applying network segmentation policy"
	# Idempotent chain setup.
	iptables -N SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT 2>/dev/null || true
	iptables -F SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT
	iptables -D INPUT -j SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT 2>/dev/null || true
	iptables -I INPUT 1 -j SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT

	# IPv6 equivalent chain setup.
	if command -v ip6tables >/dev/null 2>&1; then
		ip6tables -N SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT 2>/dev/null || true
		ip6tables -F SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT
		ip6tables -D INPUT -j SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT 2>/dev/null || true
		ip6tables -I INPUT 1 -j SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT
		
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -i lo -j ACCEPT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp --dport "$ADMIN_PORT" -s ::1/128 -j ACCEPT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp --dport "$ADMIN_PORT" -j REJECT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -i "$WG_INTERFACE_NAME" -p tcp -m multiport --dports "$PROXY_PORT","$TPROXY_PORT" -j ACCEPT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp -m multiport --dports "$PROXY_PORT","$TPROXY_PORT" -j REJECT
		ip6tables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -j RETURN
	fi

	# Baseline allows.
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -m conntrack --ctstate ESTABLISHED,RELATED -j ACCEPT
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -i lo -j ACCEPT

	# Admin/control-plane must remain host-local only.
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp --dport "$ADMIN_PORT" -s 127.0.0.1/32 -j ACCEPT
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp --dport "$ADMIN_PORT" -j REJECT

	# Data-plane ports must arrive through WireGuard interface only.
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -i "$WG_INTERFACE_NAME" -p tcp -m multiport --dports "$PROXY_PORT","$TPROXY_PORT" -j ACCEPT
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -p tcp -m multiport --dports "$PROXY_PORT","$TPROXY_PORT" -j REJECT

	# Leave all other traffic untouched for explicit deployment policy control.
	iptables -A SSL_PROXY_SEGMENT -j RETURN
	echo "[#] Network segmentation policy active (admin_bind=${ADMIN_BIND_ADDR}:${ADMIN_PORT})"
}
